orgDesign.companyProfile: Pan-European bike mobility platform business operating employer programs, rider journeys, dealer ecosystems, lifecycle finance, and emerging pooled fleet offers. The domain is modeled as a network of focused teams sized around 8-11 FTE.
orgDesign.operatingModel: Customer-facing stream teams own employer, rider, partner, and fleet outcomes, while platform teams provide identity, workflow, data, and reliability capabilities reused across markets and brands.
designPrinciples: Keep employer, rider, partner, finance, and fleet value streams explicit because they have different buyers, workflows, and economics.
Treat service, claims, and reconciliation as product areas with accountable teams rather than generic back-office support.
Use shared platform teams only where reuse is structurally high: workflow, data, and reliability.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 8-11 FTE with clear KPI ownership and a narrow enough scope to keep delivery and discovery fast.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Use top-level groups for major value streams and keep most groups to one or two teams unless the workflow clearly splits further.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: Add lightweight group-level leadership and principal IC coverage across larger value streams rather than oversizing any individual team.

groups.name: Bike Employer Growth
groups.mission: Own employer acquisition, rollout, policy setup, and the integration path that turns signed employers into live programs.

teams.name: Bike Employer Platform
teams.mission: Own employer-facing launch, policy, and KPI workflows that make bike programs easier to configure and expand.

relatedKPIs: Net revenue retention from employer programs

roles.responsibility: Own roadmap and KPI tradeoffs for employer launch and policy flows.
Lead engineering execution and team health.
Build program setup, policy, and business workflow services.
Build the employer admin and KPI experiences.
Own quality depth and release confidence.
Shape employer setup and admin workflow quality.
Bridge product decisions with employer rollout realities.

teams.name: Bike Employer Integrations
teams.mission: Own the HRIS, payroll, SSO, and employer-system integration layer required for repeatable launches.

roles.responsibility: Own integration priorities and reusable connector strategy.
Lead delivery and maintain a healthy integration estate.
Build and maintain connectors, APIs, and event flows.
Own contract testing and integration reliability.
Bridge employer landscapes with reusable domain patterns.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, architecture coherence, and launch leadership across employer growth teams.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own the employer portfolio and rollout cadence while a group-level staff engineer and specialist roles align architecture and implementation standards across teams.

roles.responsibility: Own strategy, tradeoffs, and KPI accountability across employer acquisition, launch, and integration workflows.
Own engineering execution, delivery quality, and staffing across the employer growth group.
Provide cross-team technical direction for employer workflows, integrations, and reusable launch architecture.
Drive cross-team UX coherence for employer setup, admin, and rollout workflows.
Shape shared rollout standards, integration patterns, and employer-domain semantics used by the group.

groups.name: Bike Rider Experience
groups.mission: Own rider conversion, mobile experience, post-order confidence, and lifecycle engagement.

teams.name: Bike Rider Growth
teams.mission: Own quote, selection, checkout, and first-order conversion for riders.

roles.responsibility: Own rider conversion and activation outcomes.
Lead delivery for quote and checkout workflows.
Build pricing, checkout, and order workflow services.

Own conversion-path reliability and release quality.
Design rider journey clarity and decision support.

teams.name: Bike Rider Mobile
teams.mission: Own native rider mobile experiences and the app surfaces that maintain confidence after checkout.

roles.responsibility: Own rider mobile outcomes and app roadmap.
Lead mobile delivery and execution quality.
Build and evolve iOS and Android rider experiences.
Support app-specific APIs and status services.
Own mobile regression and release confidence.
Design high-trust mobile flows and information clarity.

teams.name: Bike Rider Lifecycle
teams.mission: Own notifications, lifecycle state visibility, renewal readiness, and contract confidence after the initial order.

roles.responsibility: Own lifecycle engagement and renewal readiness outcomes.
Lead delivery for lifecycle workflows and messaging.
Build lifecycle workflow, notification, and contract-state services.
Surface lifecycle updates in rider-facing channels.
Own lifecycle flow quality and release confidence.
Design trust-building lifecycle journeys.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, architecture coherence, and journey leadership across rider-facing teams.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own rider outcomes and operating cadence while group-level staff roles align commerce, mobile, and lifecycle experience standards across teams.

roles.responsibility: Own rider strategy, funnel tradeoffs, and KPI accountability across conversion, mobile, and lifecycle teams.
Own engineering execution, staffing quality, and system health across rider experience teams.
Provide cross-team technical direction for rider-commerce, app, and lifecycle architecture.
Drive end-to-end rider journey coherence and experience quality across teams.
Shape shared funnel semantics, experimentation standards, and lifecycle measurement used by the group.

groups.name: Bike Partner and Service Operations

teams.name: Bike Partner Network

primaryCustomers.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator

roles.responsibility: Own partner coverage and order readiness outcomes.
Lead partner-platform delivery and operations coordination.
Build partner workflow and inventory services.
Build partner portal workflows.
Own partner workflow quality.

teams.mission: Own maintenance, repair, and service workflows that determine rider uptime and partner throughput.

primaryCustomers.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator

roles.responsibility: Own rider uptime and service throughput outcomes.
Lead service workflow delivery.
Build case orchestration, maintenance, and authorization services.
Own service workflow quality and regression confidence.

teams.name: Bike Claims Operations
teams.mission: Own insurance and claims workflows so riders, dealers, and finance teams can resolve incidents with less friction.

roles.responsibility: Own claims speed, cost, and experience tradeoffs.
Lead claims-platform delivery.
Build claims, evidence, and approval workflow services.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, architecture coherence, and operating leadership for partner, service, and claims workflows.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own the portfolio and service posture while group-level staff roles align partner operations, service flows, and claims architecture across teams.

roles.responsibility: Own strategy, service tradeoffs, and outcome accountability across partner and post-order operations.
Own engineering execution, staffing quality, and system health across partner and service teams.
Provide cross-team technical direction for partner connectivity, service orchestration, and claims workflows.
Drive workflow coherence across dealer, repair, support, and claims experiences.
Shape the shared partner-service operating model, case semantics, and operational handoff standards used by the group.

groups.name: Bike Finance and Lifecycle Value
groups.mission: Own financial integrity, contract changes, and lifecycle economics across the rider and employer base.

teams.name: Bike Finance Reconciliation

relatedKPIs: Net revenue retention from employer programs

primaryCustomers.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
relatedKPIs: Dealer gross margin per completed bike order

Lead finance-platform delivery and operational quality.
Build billing, payout, reconciliation, and ledger services.
Own confidence in finance-critical paths.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, financial-model coherence, and lifecycle value leadership for finance workflows.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own the financial portfolio and operating cadence while group-level staff roles align reconciliation, risk, and lifecycle-value standards.

roles.responsibility: Own strategy, economic tradeoffs, and KPI accountability across finance and lifecycle value workflows.
Own engineering execution, controls, and staffing quality across the finance group.
Provide cross-team technical direction for reconciliation, contract, and financial workflow architecture.
Shape the shared financial model, controls semantics, and lifecycle value logic used by the group.

groups.name: Bike Fleet Solutions
groups.mission: Own pooled bike and operational fleet workflows as an emerging second growth line.

teams.name: Bike Fleet Operations
teams.mission: Own pooled fleet setup, booking, assignment, and operational reporting for fleet customers.

primaryCustomers.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager

roles.responsibility: Own fleet utilization and expansion outcomes.
Lead fleet-product delivery.
Build booking, assignment, and fleet-state services.
Build fleet admin and fleet-user interfaces.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, architecture coherence, and operating leadership for fleet offers and pooled mobility workflows.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own fleet outcomes while group-level staff roles align operational, commercial, and telematics standards across the fleet teams.

roles.responsibility: Own strategy, tradeoffs, and KPI accountability across fleet operations and pooled-mobility offers.

groups.name: Bike Platform Foundation

teams.name: Bike Workflow Platform

roles.responsibility: Own shared workflow-platform priorities.

Own confidence in core shared services.

teams.name: Bike Data Platform
teams.mission: Own the data foundation for KPI reporting, unit economics, and operational decision-making.

roles.responsibility: Own KPI semantics and data-product priorities.
Lead data-platform execution.
Build trusted metric layers and reporting models.
Build pipelines, transformation logic, and data reliability controls.

teams.name: Bike Platform Reliability
teams.mission: Own observability, runtime reliability, security controls, and identity foundations for the bike-mobility platform.

roles.responsibility: Lead reliability and security engineering execution.
Shape runtime architecture, resilience, and control patterns.
Own observability, operations, and incident readiness.
Own identity, access, and platform security controls.
Translate audit and privacy obligations into platform controls.

groupLeadership.purpose: Provide cross-team direction, platform architecture coherence, and reliability leadership for shared bike-mobility foundations.
groupLeadership.leadershipModel: Directors own platform priorities and service posture while group-level staff roles align workflow, data, and reliability standards across teams.

roles.responsibility: Own shared-platform priorities, adoption tradeoffs, and service-level expectations across the bike domain.
Own engineering execution, resilience, and staffing quality across platform foundation teams.
Provide cross-team technical direction for workflow, data, API, and reliability platforms.
Shape shared service contracts, interoperability patterns, and platform standards used by all teams.
Drive telemetry semantics, analytical consistency, and reliability posture across the platform estate.
